text: The atmosphere here feels cold and deserted. The sharp angles and dim lighting prompt a certain wariness in me. I am drawn to the glow of the emergency lights as beacons in unfamiliar territory, yet I sense an undercurrent of anticipation, as if I am on the cusp of insight or discovery. The darkness amplifies my awareness of my own presence, making me question the meaning of solitude in such a constructed, utilitarian space.
